 {"id":"frankenredis-23c","title":"Track per-client peer address for CLIENT LIST/KILL accuracy","description":"## Problem

CLIENT LIST always reports addr=127.0.0.1:0. Redis reports the actual peer address (e.g., addr=10.0.0.5:54321). This breaks:
- Client monitoring tools that parse CLIENT LIST
- CLIENT KILL by addr (which needs the real address)
- Debugging connection issues in production

## Implementation Plan

1. In fr-server accept_connections, after accepting a TcpStream, call stream.peer_addr()
2. Store the SocketAddr in ClientConnection (new field: peer_addr: SocketAddr)
3. Add peer_addr to ClientSession so it's available during execute_frame
4. In fr-runtime CLIENT LIST handler, use session.peer_addr instead of hardcoded value
5. Also store the local listen address for the laddr field

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs: Store peer_addr on accept, propagate to session
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: ClientSession peer_addr field, CLIENT LIST format","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:14.087262558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:36.969306Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:36.968812907Z","close_reason":"ClientSession.peer_addr stores real peer address from accept(), CLIENT LIST uses it instead of hardcoded 127.0.0.1:0","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-23c","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-wvy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:23.199127900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Maintain a per-key counter in fr-store that drop_expired_hash_fields / drop_hash_field_if_expired increment. Reset on DEBUG OBJECT read (or DEL).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:25:03.152891361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:33:20.697191612Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:33:20.695319720Z","close_reason":"Landed in 29d0229: Store.hash_field_expired_counts BTreeMap incremented on every per-field TTL reap (bulk + targeted), cleared on whole-key removal; DEBUG OBJECT reply appends hexpired_fields:<n>. 1 new test; fr-command 626/0; fr-store workspace green; clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-29c7","title":"Add RESP parser fuzz test for crash resilience on malformed input","description":"# RESP Parser Fuzz Test

## What
A property-based test that feeds random/malformed bytes to the RESP parser and verifies it never panics, only returns Ok or Err.

## Why
The RESP parser is the first code that touches untrusted network input. A panic crashes the entire server. Property-based testing (proptest) is the practical approach since cargo-fuzz requires nightly and special setup.

## Implementation
1. Add proptest as a dev-dependency to fr-protocol
2. New test in crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs test module:
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn parse_frame_never_panics(data: Vec<u8>) {
           let _ = parse_frame(&data); // Ok or Err, never panic
       }
   }
   ```
3. Also test parse_frame_with_config with various ParserConfig settings
4. Also test with structured semi-valid input: valid RESP prefix byte + random payload
5. Run for at least 10,000 cases (proptest default is 256, increase with PROPTEST_CASES env)
6. Fix any panics found

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-protocol -- parse_frame_never_panics` passes
- Run with PROPTEST_CASES=100000 in CI

## Files
- crates/fr-protocol/Cargo.toml (add proptest dev-dependency)
- crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs (add proptest tests)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:50.074946085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:17:52.736277717Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:17:52.736003488Z","close_reason":"Added 4 proptest fuzz tests to fr-protocol: parse_frame_never_panics, parse_frame_with_config_never_panics, parse_frame_with_tight_limits_never_panics, parse_frame_with_resp_prefix_never_panics. Each runs 10,000 random input cases. All pass — zero panics found. Added proptest dev-dependency.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-2auy","title":"Add minimal client session context to Store for CLIENT stubs in dispatch_argv","description":"# Add minimal client context to Store for CLIENT command in dispatch_argv

## Problem
CLIENT ID/GETNAME/SETNAME/LIST/INFO in fr-command return hardcoded values (id=1, name=nil, etc.) because they don't have access to the ClientSession. Lua scripts calling redis.call(\"CLIENT\", \"ID\") get 1 instead of their real client ID.

## Design
Add a \"current client context\" to Store that the runtime populates before dispatching:
```rust
pub struct DispatchClientContext {
    pub client_id: u64,
    pub client_name: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    pub db_index: usize,
    pub flags: String,   // e.g., \"N\" for normal
    pub peer_addr: String,
}
```

The runtime already swaps sessions before dispatch — it can populate this context at the same time.

## Implementation
1. Add pub dispatch_client_ctx: DispatchClientContext to Store
2. In Runtime::execute_frame (before dispatch_argv call), populate store.dispatch_client_ctx from current session
3. Update fr-command's client_cmd to read from store.dispatch_client_ctx:
   - CLIENT ID: return ctx.client_id
   - CLIENT GETNAME: return ctx.client_name
   - CLIENT SETNAME: update ctx.client_name (runtime will read it back)
   - CLIENT LIST/INFO: format using ctx fields
4. After dispatch_argv returns, runtime reads back any changes (e.g., SETNAME)

## Scope limitation
CLIENT LIST in Redis returns ALL connected clients. Through dispatch_argv, we can only report the current client. This is acceptable for Lua scripts (they typically only need their own info). The runtime's CLIENT LIST already returns all clients.

## Depends on
- No hard dependencies, but nice to land after the counter foundation

## Testing
- Conformance: Lua script calls redis.call(\"CLIENT\", \"ID\") and gets a real ID (not 1)
- Unit test: populate context, verify CLIENT GETNAME returns it

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (DispatchClientContext struct, Store field)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (client_cmd reads context)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (populate context before dispatch)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:41.559944609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:26:45.359816290Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:26:45.359474203Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-7o5","title":"[EPIC] Stream and Persistence Completeness","description":"## Overview

Several stream commands have accuracy gaps, and the persistence layer has known limitations that affect data integrity and Redis interoperability.

## Stream Issues

1. XINFO CONSUMERS returns hardcoded zeros for pending count, idle time, and inactive time instead of computing real values from the PEL.

2. XPENDING extended form doesn't support the IDLE option (Redis 6.2+). Commands like \"XPENDING key group IDLE 5000 - + 10\" return WrongArity errors.

3. XINFO STREAM radix-tree metrics are hardcoded to 1/0 instead of real values.

## Persistence Issues

4. DUMP uses CRC16 (2 bytes) instead of Redis's CRC64 (8 bytes). This means:
   - DUMP/RESTORE payloads are not interoperable with Redis
   - Only 16 bits of integrity protection (trivially forgeable)

5. DUMP/RESTORE bounds checks for non-String types use payload.len() instead of payload.len()-11, allowing the parser to read into the trailer bytes.

## Previously Fixed (for reference)

- XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM idle time <= vs < (fixed)
- Stream last-ID watermark (fixed)
- XREADGROUP pending replay delivery count (fixed)
- Stream RDB persistence (fixed — streams now saved/loaded)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:10.066810608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.516049261Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.515529597Z","close_reason":"Epic acknowledged — children tasks now unblocked for implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankenredis-t2nh","title":"rv89 part 4c: HGETEX + HGETDEL commands","description":"Implement HGETEX and HGETDEL (Redis 7.4) in fr-command:

  HGETEX key [EX s | PX ms | EXAT unix-s | PXAT unix-ms | PERSIST] FIELDS n field ...
  HGETDEL key FIELDS n field ...

Returns array of bulk strings (values or nil per field). HGETEX optionally sets/removes field TTLs as part of the read. HGETDEL deletes fields after reading.

Reuse the parse_hash_field_ttl_flags_and_fields_header helper from part 2.
Wire through is_write_command for HGETDEL (mutates) and HGETEX when TTL-modifying flags are present.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:25:02.983207257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:30:44.618596474Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:30:44.616307923Z","close_reason":"Landed in 67bf107: HGETEX (EX/PX/EXAT/PXAT/PERSIST) + HGETDEL commands routed through CommandId + classifier + is_write_command. 6 new tests; fr-command 625/0; workspace clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-tek","title":"Fix O(N^2) DoS vulnerability in BITOP","description":"The BITOP command evaluated the operation type (AND, OR, XOR) inside the inner byte-processing loop. With string lengths up to 512 MiB, this caused a massive CPU bottleneck. Hoisted the operation check outside the loop to ensure O(N) time complexity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T02:30:13.942355423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T02:30:27.947588648Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T02:30:27.946691198Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
